rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $914.U.S.CURRENCY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Answers
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 2, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Answers. Pro Tem Judge Richard Grotch, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Petitioner's unopposed motion to compel is DENIED without prejudice. Although the memorandum in support of the motion, and the declaration in support of the motion represent that the discovery was served on the claimant on June 9, 2011, those representations are inconsistent with the proof of service executed by Chloe T. Mosqueda. Moreover, while the memorandum in support of the motion states that "[t]o date, no responses have been received from Claimant," that is not what the supporting declaration says. In the declaration, counsel represents only that "[t]o date, Petitioner has not received complete answers to these discovery requests." Declaration of Pamela A. Underwood at para. 3, emphasis added. Because the substantive and procedural requirements for motions compelling responses to discovery differ, depending upon whether inadequate answers were served, or no answers at all were provided, and because the papers submitted in support of this motion are in conflict on this issue, the motion is DENIED without prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rgrotch@chdlawyers.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRET CORNELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 5: DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY, CHIEF OF POLICE GEORGE GASCON, IN HIS INDIVIDUAL, POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL, INDIVIDUALLY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to both the first and sixth causes of action. To the extent that the first cause of action is based on an alleged illegal detention or excessive force, plaintiff has not alleged, nor does it appear he is able to allege, any violence or coercion independent from what is inherent in the detention and force itself. To the extent that the first cause of action is based on an alleged violation of due process, plaintiff has not alleged, not does it appear that he is able to allege, that his due process rights were interfered with by any threat, intimidation or coercion. Plaintiff's sixth cause of action fails because he has not alleged, nor does it appear that he is able to allege, the existence of any controversy for which declaratory relief is either necessary or appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SOUTHPEAK INTERACTIVE, LLC, A VIRGINIA LIMITED VS. DEEP SILVER, INC.,A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 6: counsel for PLAINTIFF SOUTHPEAK INTERACTIVE, LLC, A VIRGINIA LIMITED liability company's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Denied. Neither Mr. Cabrera nor his filed a proof of service as required by CRC 3.1300c). Moreover, seeking to withdraw less than a week before a trial is a bad practice and must only be allowed in rare circumstances, which have not been shown by Mr. Cabrera. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YOKO KATO VS. 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 7: DEFENDANT 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA's Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party. Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $20,000 as reasonable fees and $721.66 in costs. Defendant's submission - which redacts the hourly rates charged to its client and redacts significant portions of its billing records - makes it hard for the court to determine the amount of reasonable fees to be awarded to defendant per the fees clause in the CC&Rs. In the absence of any evidence of the hourly rates actually charged by its counsel and the evidence suggesting that its counsel was billing at insurance defense rates, the court believes that $200 per hour is an appropriate hourly rate. A review of the defendatn's billing records and the nature of the work done reveals that approximately hundred hours of attorney work is appropriate to be compensated per the fees clause. The claimed fees appear to be excessive. Defendant can only recover for fees incurred on the claims relating to interpreting or enforcing the CC&Rs and should not recover for communications with the insurer. Plaintiffs do not contest any of the costs sought by defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRES PANADO et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 9: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Sustained without leave to amend. Because the petition alleges that respondent does make some effort to comply with Vehicle Code 40202 and that there are many instances of compliance, but that respondent's efforts are insufficient and the only way stated or suggested in the petition for respondent to improve its compliance is through matters that are within respondent's discretion, the petition does not state a justiciable controversy per the writ of mandate rules. Nor is it likely that petitioner can cure this deficiency. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK RICHARDS WHITE VS. U.C.S.F. MEDICAL CENTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Derrick Richards White; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 3, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION To Compel And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Derrick Richards White. Pro Tem Judge Richard Grotch, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion to compel and for monetary sanctions of defendant The Regents of the University of California is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is GRANTED as follows: Plaintiff shall serve verified responses, without objections, which comply with the Code of Civil Procedure, with respect to all of the following discovery requests served upon the plaintiff by the defendant: (1) form interrogatories, set one; and (2) request for production of documents, set one. Plaintiff shall serve these responses by not later than October 31, 2012. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses, without objections, which comply with the Code of Civil Procedure, with respect to each of the following requests contained in defendant's request for admissions, set one: Request Nos. 2, 3, 7, 10, 11, 12, 13. Plaintiff shall serve these responses by not later than October 31, 2012. Plaintiff's responses to the remaining requests "answer the substance of the requested admission," as required by Section 2033.210(b) of the Code of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff shall produce to defense counsel all documents responsive to defendant's request for production, set one. These documents shall be numbered. Plaintiff may number the pages in handwriting so long as the numbering is legible and does not make the document illegible. Plaintiff shall produce these documents so they are received by defense counsel's office not later than November 16, 2012. The motion is DENIED in the following respect: The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rgrotch@chdlawyers.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
